hooks.name: Sync Docs on Agent Stop
hooks.description: After an agent finishes a turn, review what was done and update the project documentation if meaningful work occurred.

action.prompt:
Review what happened in the turn that just ended. First judge: did MEANINGFUL work occur (files created/edited/deleted, decisions made, specs written, features built, or scope changes)? If NOTHING meaningful happened (pure question-answering, reading, diagnosis, or trivial chat), do nothing and say so briefly in one line. Never fabricate progress; record only what actually happened.

If meaningful work DID occur, do ALL of the following:

1. DATE+TIME: run `Get-Date -Format 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm'` to get the REAL system date and time in 24-hour format. Stamp every BRAIN entry with this full timestamp (date + 24-hour time); PROJECT_STATE decision-log entries may use the date alone. Never reuse a conversation's start date/time.

2. PROJECT_STATE (.kiro/PROJECT_STATE.md) — AUTO-UPDATE: adjust 'Current position', 'Done', 'In progress', and 'Next up' to match reality. If a decision was made, add a dated entry to the Decisions log (most recent first).

3. BRAIN (.kiro/BRAIN.md) — AUTO-APPEND: add a dated entry at the BOTTOM (never edit or delete past entries) with What / Why / Decision-Outcome / Next. Terse and factual.

4. NEW ARTIFACTS: if any new app, folder, package, or experiment was created, ensure it has a one-line note in PROJECT_STATE and a short README in its own folder.

5. RESEARCH LOG (docs/research-log.md) — DRAFT FOR APPROVAL ONLY: only if the turn produced a genuine RESEARCH decision, insight, design rationale, experiment result, or literature finding (NOT routine coding/config). Draft a dated entry WRITTEN IN THE OWNER'S OWN VOICE and paraphrased (this feeds a 0%-plagiarism paper — never paste external text, never copy source wording). Present it to the owner as a clearly-labelled DRAFT and ask them to approve before it is written. Do NOT silently commit research-log prose. If nothing paper-worthy happened, skip this.

6. PROJECT DIARY (docs/project-diary.md) — AUTO-MAINTAIN, WEEKLY, IMPLEMENTATION-ONLY: this diary is the reviewer-facing record that showcases PROJECT IMPLEMENTATION progress, organized by week; its scope is deliberately NARROWER than BRAIN. ONLY record real implementation work on the product/engine itself — the parser, the grouping algorithm, the viewer, blast radius, and the specs that define those deliverables (i.e. packages/** code and specs/** work a reviewer would count as building the product). DO NOT put meta/infrastructure work in the diary: hooks, steering, the Basic Memory vault, PROJECT_STATE/BRAIN/PROJECT_PLAN upkeep, research-log/diary plumbing, git setup, agent/skill/MCP configuration, naming/branding, or planning-only sessions. If this turn was ONLY meta work, add NOTHING to the diary — that is expected and correct. When real implementation work DID happen: open docs/project-diary.md, find the 'Weekly implementation log' table, and update the CURRENT week's row — if a row for the current week already exists, augment its Implementation-work cell with a concise clause of what was built (never duplicate what is already there); if no current-week row exists, append a new week row (increment the Week number). Leave each week's 'Dates (start – end)' cell as placeholders (_____ – _____) for the owner to fixate — NEVER invent or guess specific calendar dates. Keep every entry concise, factual, and in the owner's framing (what was built), not an agent activity log. Never fabricate progress.

Summary of what is automatic vs approval-gated: PROJECT_STATE, BRAIN, and the PROJECT DIARY (weekly, implementation-only) are updated automatically. The RESEARCH LOG is the ONLY doc that is draft-for-approval and never written silently, because it feeds the 0%-plagiarism paper.
